diagrams.title: Imbalanced Dataset with Outliers
diagrams.description: Visualizes a 2D scatter plot showing class imbalance between two classes (majority and minority), with outliers clearly marked. The majority class forms a dense cluster while the minority class is sparse with points scattered around. Outliers are shown as isolated points far from both clusters.
elements: Legend showing class labels and outliers
diagrams.purpose: Helps learners understand what an imbalanced dataset looks like and how outliers coexist with class imbalance, setting the foundation for why class balancing and outlier detection are needed together.

diagrams.title: Class Balancing Techniques Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows three panels comparing different class balancing approaches: (1) Original imbalanced distribution, (2) Oversampling with synthetic points (SMOTE-style) generating new minority samples between existing ones, (3) Undersampling removing majority class points. Arrows indicate the transformation process.
elements: Oversampled dataset with synthetic points (different color/pattern)
Undersampled dataset with reduced majority points
diagrams.purpose: Explains the two main approaches to handling class imbalance - oversampling and undersampling - and shows how synthetic samples are generated, which is essential for understanding how to prepare data for outlier detection.

diagrams.title: Outlier Detection Methods Pipeline
diagrams.description: Illustrates a machine learning pipeline showing three outlier detection approaches: (1) Statistical method using Z-score with normal distribution curve and threshold lines, (2) Density-based method (LOF/DBSCAN) showing local density differences, (3) Isolation Forest showing how outliers are isolated with shorter path lengths. All methods identify and flag outliers.
elements: Z-score method with normal distribution and threshold
Density-based method with reachability distance visualization
Isolation Forest with random partitioning tree visualization
Output: flagged outliers highlighted in red
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates multiple outlier detection techniques that can be applied after class balancing, showing how each method identifies anomalies differently and which metrics evaluate their performance.
